items.title: How the U.S.-Iran deal set the stage for renewed fighting over the Strait of Hormuz
items.description: The U.S. and Iran's contradictory interperations of the memorandum of understanding have led to renewed fighting over the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: Stablecoin issuer Circle just got the greenlight to operate as a bank. The shares are up 5%
items.description: Stablecoin issuer Circle surged in premarket trading after the U.S. Office of the Comptroller of the Currency granted it approval to operate as a trust bank.

items.title: As bank earnings approach, a market anomaly emerges
items.description: The Financial Select Sector Index trades at roughly 15.5 times forward earnings — about a turn and a quarter cheaper than where it stood in 2024.

items.title: Air taxi company Beta wraps first test flights in U.S. government's pilot program
items.description: Beta is one of several electric air taxi makers racing to secure Federal Aviation Administration certification and start flying passengers commercially.

items.title: SK Hynix opens at $170 on Nasdaq. Chairman tells CNBC 'demand is enormous'
items.description: SK Hynix has soared to a trillion-dollar market cap by serving some of the biggest names in technology, including Nvidia and Apple.

items.title: Senate Democrats want hearings on Trump’s crypto holdings and foreign investors
items.description: Democratic Senators cited $1.4 billion in Trump family crypto income, unidentified investors, and a reported UAE-backed stake in World Liberty Financial.

items.title: We're looking to buy more of this chip stock as the once-hot group cools
items.description: The Investing Club holds its "Morning Meeting" every weekday at 10:20 a.m. ET.

items.title: Trump says U.S. to continue talks with Iran despite scrapped ceasefire
items.description: The ceasefire signed last month came under serious strain in recent days as U.S. and Iranian forces exchanged attacks for consecutive days this week.

items.title: Trump purges Election Assistance Commission members, months before midterms
items.description: The White House said the Supreme Court's ruling allowing President Trump to fire FTC Commissioner Louise Slaughter provided the precedent for the removals.

items.title: Meta's stock heads for best week since early 2024 as optimism builds around AI strategy
items.description: Meta released two AI models this week as part of its new Muse Spark family, under the leadership of Alexandr Wang.

items.title: Kraken is rebuilding its app around agentic trading as crypto exchanges evolve beyond crypto
items.description: Kraken is rebuilding its app around agentic trading, the company told CNBC exclusively.

items.title: New Fed task force members share Chairman Kevin Warsh's embrace of AI
items.description: The three members of a task force advising the Fed on artificial intelligence are strong proponents of the technology.

items.title: AI, data center fears could be key in closely watched Michigan Democratic Senate primary
items.description: Abdul El-Sayed and Rep. Haley Stevens will face off in the Michigan Democratic Senate primary on Aug. 4.

items.title: Private chef salaries reach $300,000 as the rich seek their own Michelin stars
items.description: Demand for chefs, personal assistants, butlers, nannies, housekeepers, chauffeurs and estate managers have reached records, according to Morgan & Mallet.

items.title: These are America's 10 cheapest states for 2026, where you can still beat inflation
items.description: These are America's cheapest states to live in for 2026, where residents can still beat inflation.

items.title: Trump says he won't sign housing bill, which would become law automatically
items.description: Congress passed the bill that aims to address the costs of homes and rein in institutional investors with strong bipartisan support in June.

items.title: These are America's 10 most expensive states for 2026, where inflation is punishing residents
items.description: These 10 U.S. states are the most expensive places to live in America in 2026, with inflation punishing residents to an extreme degree.

items.title: 48-year-old worked at a garden center for $17/hour to make ends meet: 'It's one of the best jobs I've ever had'
items.description: "Without a job, I felt invisible. With a job, I felt seen, useful, and purposeful again," says Leslie Friday, who sold plants and flowers while job searching after a layoff. "I loved every minute."

items.title: Ukraine escalates attacks on tankers near Crimea as Russian fuel shortages bite
items.description: The drone strikes form part of Ukraine's campaign designed to choke off supplies and transportation routes in and out of Crimea.

items.title: Why the stock market and economy may seem out of sync
items.description: The stock market has boomed on AI euphoria, while the trajectory of the U.S. economy has been more tepid, economists said.
